questions.guidance: Accept e.g. 2, 7; 2, 2, 7. Do not accept e.g. $2 \times 7$; $2^2 \times 7$; 1, 2, 7

markAllocation.criterion: for $3y^2$ or $5y$ correct in final answer or for correct answer seen then spoilt

questions.guidance: B1 may be equivalent answer in factorised form. For B1 accept e.g. $4 + 5y$
If 0 scored, SC1 for their more accurate value seen rounded correctly to 1 decimal place
Mark answer space first. If answer space blank then mark diagram and working. or B1 for $[C =] (7.5, p)$ or $(q, 3)$, $q \neq 8$ or $[D =] (9.5, r)$ or $(s, 6.5)$

Accept answer 566 if more accurate value seen. M2 for e.g. $12 \times 45.5 + 0.3 \times 12 \times 5.5$. Allow M1 for method seen even if not used or spoilt. or M1 for $12 \times 40$ oe or $12 \times 45.5$. or $[5.5 \times] 12 \times (1 + \frac{30}{100})$ oe. or $[12 \times] 5.5 \times (1 + \frac{30}{100})$ oe. or $12 \times 0.3 \times 5.5$. implied by 480 or 546. implied by 15.6[0] or 85.8. implied by 7.15 oe. implied by 19.8 oe

parts.label: (a)
parts.guidance: Accuracy $\pm$ ½ small square radially. Use overlay as a guide

parts.label: (b)
parts.guidance: Ignore embellishments e.g. strong

parts.label: (c)
parts.guidance: Use overlay for tolerance. Line must be ruled and solid and pass through gate at (8, 4.3) to (8, 4.7) and touch or pass through gates at (2, 2.6) to (2, 3.3) and (15, 5.9) to (15, 6.6). Condone if 2 mm short horizontally of reaching both outer gates provided it is correct when extended or allow slight slip at 2 mm from end[s] of ruled lines if still in tolerance

parts.label: (d)
parts.answer: FT reading at 14 weeks from their straight line of best fit with positive gradient
parts.guidance: Allow for intention of a straight line/dashed line. Tolerance $\pm$ 0.05 of their reading at 14

parts.label: (a)
parts.guidance: or M1 for answer their written measurement $\times$ 0.5 correctly evaluated. Accept answers 2 to 2.2 [km]. For B1 accept 4 to 4.4 [cm]. For M1 their measurement must lie between 4 cm and 5 cm

parts.label: (b)
parts.answer: Correct construction for position of L with intersecting arcs 8.8 cm from A and 6.6 cm from B

markAllocation.criterion: for correct position of L indicated with no/incorrect arcs or for completely correct with intersecting arcs but L to the west of A and B

parts.guidance: Tolerance $\pm$ 2 mm for all marks — use ruler tools to measure. Accept any clear unambiguous indication of L, e.g. a dot, the intersection of arcs. or B1 for 8.8 cm from A or 6.6 cm from B soi. Accept e.g. an arc 8.8 cm from A. If 0 scored, SC1 for fully correct with arcs but distances reversed 6.6 cm from A and 8.8 cm from B or for position of L to the east with intersecting arcs 4.4 cm from A and 3.3 cm from B

parts.label: (a)
parts.guidance: Condone trailing zeros in (a) and (b)

subparts.label: (a)(i)

markAllocation.criterion: for reflection in $y = k$ or for reflection in $x = 1$

subparts.guidance: Mark intention, accept freehand, use overlay as a guide in part (a)(i) and (ii)

parts.label: (b)
parts.guidance: More than one transformation scores zero. Not turn. Accept 90, 270 clockwise, $-270$, 90 counterclockwise, 90 a/c. Condone omission of brackets in coords, accept 'position vector' $\binom{3}{-1}$ but not vector $\binom{3}{-1}$. If the centre has been described as a vector (word or bracket notation) then do not treat as additional transformation

parts.label: (a)
parts.guidance: Mark at most accurate. For 2 marks accept 1840, 1842, 1843, 1842.5, 1842.6, 1842.59... M1 may be done in stages and method can be implied by values given to 3 sf or better from previous stage[s]. $1500 \times (1 + 4.2\%)^5$ is not enough for M1. Method spoiled if e.g. $1500 + 1500 \times (1 + \frac{4.2}{100})^5$ but allow M1 if $1500 \times (1 + \frac{4.2}{100})^5 - 1500$. M1 implied by answer 343, 342.5, 342.6, 342.59...

parts.label: (b)
parts.guidance: Mark at most accurate. M2 implied by 1.065[0...] or 106.5[0...] or 0.065[0...]. or 1.07 nfww or 107 nfww. or M1 for $[k \times] y^{11} = 2[\times k]$ oe. e.g. uses a value or letter for the investment $1500 y^{11} = 3000$. M2 not for e.g. $\sqrt[11]{\frac{2y}{y}}$

questions.guidance: Mark at most accurate. Accept $85\pi$ as final answer. Allow 3.14 or $\frac{22}{7}$ for $\pi$ for M1. Answer in the wider range 266.9 to 267.1... not scoring 2 marks, implies M1

questions.answer:
$n^3 + 4$ oe final answer
and
$\frac{11 - n}{n + 2}$ oe final answer
and
$2^{n+1}$ oe final answer
questions.guidance: For $n^3 + 4$: or B1 for any cubic expression or for 3rd diff = 6 with at least two shown or for correct answer seen then spoilt. Condone use of other variable throughout. Condone e.g. $n = n^3 + 4$ throughout. For $\frac{11-n}{n+2}$: B2 accept oe e.g. $\frac{10 - (n-1)}{3 + (n-1)}$. or B1 for fraction with numerator or denominator correct or for correct answer seen then spoilt. For $2^{n+1}$: B2 oe e.g. $2(2^n)$, $4 \times 2^{n-1}$, $(\frac{1}{2})^{-(1+n)}$. or B1 for answer of form $[a \times] 2^{n[+b]}$ or $[a \times] (\frac{1}{2})^{[b]-n}$. B0 for e.g. $(\frac{1}{2})^{n+1}$, $2^{2n}$, $4^n$

parts.label: (c)

markAllocation.criterion: for $x = 5 - 4y$ or $y - 5 = -4x$ or $\frac{y}{4} = \frac{5}{4} - x$ or better

parts.guidance: For 2 marks, accept $\frac{x - 5}{-4}$. If answer e.g. $5 - x/4$ then check working and if written correctly in working allow 2 marks, otherwise M1 maximum

parts.label: (a)

markAllocation.criterion: for use of $\Sigma fm$ with $m$ in correct interval including both boundaries

parts.guidance: Mark at most accurate. Accept $1\frac{97}{160}$, 1.61, 1.606[2] or 1.6063 nfww. isw answer $1.6 < h \leq 1.7$ after correct answer seen. Condone one error or omission. Condone one further error or omission. $16.2 + 31 + 56.1 + 25.2 [= 128.5]$ (no more than two $m$ values not in interval). Accept $(12 + 20 + 34 + 14)$ for 80

parts.label: (b)
parts.guidance: or B1 for 2 correct blocks. Accuracy — bars must touch correct horizontal line for most of interval, accept freehand. Condone omission of right-hand vertical line on final block. If 0 scored, SC1 for 3 correct freq densities soi (40, 200, 340, 70)

parts.label: (a)
parts.guidance: Do not accept answer $\frac{0}{10}$

parts.label: (b)
parts.answer:
Ruled tangent to curve at time = 7.5 seconds
0.25 to 0.4

markAllocation.criterion: for ruled tangent to curve at 7.5
for gradient 0.25 to 0.4, dep on tangent correct or close attempt

parts.guidance: No daylight between tangent and curve at 7.5. Min length at least 2 cm horizontally. See AG. Accept equivalent proper fractions if in range

parts.label: (c)
parts.guidance: Accept equivalent mixed number answers. Accept answer 6.6 or 6.66 only after $\frac{100}{15}$ oe. M2 implied by improper fraction $\frac{100}{15}$ oe. or M1 for attempt at one relevant area under graph for time between 15 and 30 seconds. M1 implied by 80 to 82 or 19 to 20. Condone slight scale misread $\pm$ 0.05 when finding area e.g. $10.5 \times 8$

parts.label: (a)

markAllocation.criterion: for North line at C with angle 35 or 145 marked on diagram at C isw or for $360 - 35 - 38$ oe

parts.guidance: M1 implied by answer 73 or 73 marked at C as anticlockwise angle from North to CB isw

parts.label: (b)

markAllocation.criterion: for correct substitution into cosine rule

parts.guidance: M2 may be done in steps, allow if square root shown after error provided correct substitution is shown. M1 for $65^2 + 95^2 - 2 \times 65 \times 95 \cos 38$. Accept $13\ 250 - 12\ 350 \cos 38$ as minimum for M2 or M1. If M1 and then 59.31..., allow M2A1. See AG

parts.label: (c)
parts.guidance: Mark at most accurate. Accept answer 244 or 245. For B3 accept answer 225 or 226 if more accurate value seen. OR M2 for $[\sin A =] \frac{95 \sin 38}{59.3}$ oe or $[\cos A =] \frac{59.3^2 + 65^2 - 95^2}{2 \times 59.3 \times 65}$. or M1 for $\frac{59.3}{\sin 38} = \frac{95}{\sin A}$ oe or $95^2 = 59.3^2 + 65^2 - 2 \times 59.3 \times 65 \cos A$ oe. M1dep for $145 + their\ BAC$ leading to answer. Dep on at least M1. For M2 or M1 allow more accurate value for 59.3 (condone 59) and allow longer methods. M2 for complete method to find angle A. M2 implied by [angle $BAC$] = 80.40 to 80.51. If longer method and find angle B first then angle $B$ = 42.4 or 42.42 to 42.44... Allow method for angle A if seen in part (a) or by the diagram and used in part (c). See AG

parts.label: (a)

markAllocation.criterion: for $2(9a^2 - 49)$ or $(6a - 14)(3a + 7)$ or $(6a + 14)(3a - 7)$ isw or for correct answer seen and spoilt

parts.guidance: Condone additional superfluous brackets. Condone omission of final bracket. Accept answer $2(7 - 3a)(-7 - 3a)$. M1 for $(3\sqrt{2}a - 7\sqrt{2})(3\sqrt{2}a + 7\sqrt{2})$. accept e.g. $2(3^2 a^2 - 7^2)$

parts.label: (b)

markAllocation.criterion: for correct expansion unsimplified or for answer simplified 4 term expression of correct form with 3 terms correct

parts.guidance: e.g. $2x^3 - x^2 + 8x^2 - 4x - 4x^2 + 2x - 16x + 8$. For B2 or B1 accept terms in a grid. or B1 for one pair of brackets expanded with at least 3 terms out of 4 correct. $2x^2 - x + 8x - 4$, $+7x$ counts as two terms. $2x^2 - 4x - x + 2$, $-5x$ counts as two terms. $x^2 - 2x + 4x - 8$, $+2x$ counts as two terms. If in a larger train of random terms only accept if four correct consecutive terms given

markAllocation.criterion: for one correct angle as answer

questions.guidance: Condone 114 and 246 or 113.57 to 113.58 and 246.42 to 246.43 for 3 marks or for B2. or M1 for $\cos x = -0.4$ oe. If M1 or 0 scored, SC1 for 2 angles that add and round to 360.0. Not multiples of 90

For 4 marks accept e.g. $\frac{2}{9}(7\mathbf{m} + 2\mathbf{n})$. e.g. B3 for $\mathbf{m} + \mathbf{n} + \frac{5}{9}(\mathbf{m} - \mathbf{n})$. or M2 for $\overrightarrow{AE} = \frac{4}{9}(-\mathbf{m} + \mathbf{n})$ or $\overrightarrow{EA} = \frac{4}{9}(\mathbf{m} - \mathbf{n})$ or $\overrightarrow{BE} = \frac{5}{9}(\mathbf{m} - \mathbf{n})$ or $\overrightarrow{EB} = \frac{5}{9}(\mathbf{n} - \mathbf{m})$. or M1 for $\overrightarrow{OB} = \mathbf{m} + \mathbf{n}$ or $\overrightarrow{BO} = -\mathbf{m} - \mathbf{n}$ or $\overrightarrow{AB} = \mathbf{n} - \mathbf{m}$ or $\overrightarrow{BA} = \mathbf{m} - \mathbf{n}$ or for a correct vector route along the lines on the diagram. Not just for $\overrightarrow{OE}$. Accept mixed notation e.g. $2\mathbf{m} + \frac{4}{9}AB$

markAllocation.criterion: for forming a 3-term quadratic in $x$ or $y$

questions.guidance: For M2: M1 for $4x + 12 = 2x^2 - x - 3$ or better or $y = 2(\frac{y-12}{4})^2 - (\frac{y-12}{4}) - 3$ or better. Do not FT from $2x^2 - x - 3 [= 0]$. For M2: or M1 for $\sqrt{(-5)^2 - 4(2)(-15)}$ or better or for $\frac{-(-5) + \sqrt{q}}{2 \times 2}$ oe or $\frac{-(-5) - \sqrt{q}}{2 \times 2}$ or for $(x - \frac{5}{4})^2$. For M2, do not accept $\frac{5 \pm \sqrt{145}}{4}$. Sq root to cover $-$ sign, division to cover $\pm$ sign, but can recover with working seen. For B2: B1 for one correct pair or both $x$-values correct or both $y$-values correct. Condone more accurate values for B2 and B1: $(4.260..., 29.041$ to $29.042)$ and $(-1.760..., 4.958...)$. Correct or more accurate values may be seen in working for B2 or B1. If $x = 4.26$ and $x = -1.76$ seen in working and then transcribed incorrectly to answer line as $y$ values then allow B1. Similarly if working with $y$ values first

Mark at most accurate. In this question for method marks allow 3.14 or $\frac{22}{7}$ for $\pi$. OR B2 for angle at centre = 157 or 156.9 or 156.92 to 156.93 or for [reflex angle =] 203 to 203.1. or M2 for $2 \times \cos^{-1}(\frac{3 - 2.5}{2.5})$ oe. or M1 for $\cos x = \frac{3 - 2.5}{2.5}$ oe. Implied by $x = 78.5$ or $78.46...$. M2dep for $\frac{360 - their\ \theta}{360} \times \pi \times 2.5^2$, dep $90 < \theta < 180$ and $\neq 135$. M2 implied by 11.1 or 11.06 to 11.08... M2 spoiled if e.g. area of triangle subtracted but allow M1. M1dep for $\frac{[360-]their\ \theta}{360} \times \pi \times 2.5^2$ seen, dep on $90 < \theta < 180$ and consistent with $\theta$ used for area of sector. M1 implied by 8.55 to 8.57. M1dep for $\frac{1}{2} \times 2.5 \times 2.5 \times \sin(their\ \theta)$ oe, dep $90 < \theta < 180$ and consistent with $\theta$ used for area of sector. or $2 \times 0.5 \times \sqrt{2.5^2 - 0.5^2} \times 0.5$ oe. M1 implied by $\frac{\sqrt{6}}{2}$ or 1.22 or 1.221 to 1.225. M1 for their area $\times$ 800 leading to answer. their area must include the area of a sector or area of circle with radius 2.5 cm [$\pm$ area of a triangle]. See AG
